_publications._publicationTitle: Use of machine learning to predict cognitive performance based on brain metabolism in Neurofibromatosis type 1.

_contextSnippet: Group differences with medium effects were observed in measures of...visuoconstructive abilities (ROCF copy), short-term verbal and visual episodic memory (immediate recalls of RAVLT and ROCF)
details: Demonstrated medium effect sizes for visuoconstructive abilities (copy: 26.81 ± 8.36 vs 30.38 ± 6.21, d=0.50) and immediate recall (12.94 ± 9.14 vs 17.94 ± 6.83, d=0.64) in NF1 vs controls
